concepts.title: Assassination as State Instrument
concepts.description: three forms: physical / will / character assassination; Chuan Chu (dagger in fish, ceremony as delivery mechanism); Yao Li (self-sacrifice cover); Ching K'o's failed attempt as failure analysis; beauty agents as will assassination; agent selection as chih jen application

concepts.title: Systematic Covert Programs
concepts.description: Kou-chien's nine-technique campaign against Wu: sacred timber exhaustion / beauty agents / poisoned grain / financial inducements / Wu Yüan estrangement / training programs / artisan intelligence / rebellion support / patient timing; Chieh Hsüan's mu/obfuscation doctrinal framework; Li Ch'uan's coordination-across-time

concepts.title: Signs of a Doomed State
concepts.description: Han Fei-tzu's 35+ indicators (structural / personal behavioral / absence of Worthies / social behavioral); ironclad indicator: absence of Worthies = self-confirming failure to self-correct; Wei Liao-tzu doomed-city analysis; Pai Kuei's five exhaustion signs

concepts.description: 3,000-year arc: Shang universality → Spring-Autumn selective acceptance → Wei Liao-tzu condemnation → Li Ching's cynical instrumentalization ("manipulate the greedy and stupid") → Northern Sung rationalist consolidation; strategic irrationalism as professional competency

concepts.title: Franklin Crew Forensic Analysis and Cannibalism
concepts.description: Captain James Fitzjames DNA identification (2014); cut marks on ribs, vertebrae, long bones; systematic butchering patterns; ~25% of recovered remains showing deliberate butchering

concepts.title: Franklin Expedition Historical Narrative
concepts.description: Lead poisoning → scurvy → starvation → organized cannibalism → death sequence; ships trapped in pack ice September 1846; abandoned April 1848; crew dispersal and systematic butchering for food

concepts.title: Arctic Exploration Failure and Adaptation
concepts.description: Environmental extremity; indigenous Arctic adaptation (Inuit, Yupik, Sámi); European refusal to adopt indigenous practices; inadequate provisioning, clothing, ships, knowledge; Franklin's refusal to adopt indigenous methods

title: Chinese Civilization Origins — Shamanism to State (Stone Age Herbalist — Demon Bats & Cocaine Mummies Vol. III, 2026)

concepts.title: Shamanism and Chinese State Neolithic to Shang
concepts.description: 7,000-year arc from individual charisma (Peiligang shamanism) → institutionalized priesthood (Hongshan jade priests) → formalized divination (Longshan oracle bones) → state apparatus (Shang bureaucracy); phases: Peiligang/Shangshan (~8,000 BCE), Yangshao/Hongshan (~5,000 BCE), Longshan (~3,000 BCE), Taosi (~2,600 BCE), Shimao (~2,300 BCE), Erlitou/Erligang (~1900 BCE), Shang (~1200 BCE)

concepts.title: Peiligang Early Shamanism
concepts.description: 8,000-7,000 BCE; pit houses, stone tools, early pottery; egalitarian society with ritual specialists; personal authority based on demonstrated efficacy; no hereditary succession; limitation of shamanic authority (non-transferable); transition to institutional priesthood

concepts.title: Hongshan Ritual Landscape and Jade
concepts.description: 5,000-3,000 BCE; jade pig-dragons, combs, bi discs, cong cylinders; ritual deposits on hilltops; goddess imagery and female ritual specialists; jade as status marker and heritable authority; shift from personal shamanic to institutional priesthood

concepts.title: Taosi City Astronomy and State
concepts.description: 300-hectare walled city; astronomical platform aligned to solstices; knowledge-based political authority; shift from shamanism to astronomy to authority based on expertise; legendary Emperor Yao connection

concepts.title: Shimao Skull Sacrifice and Ritual Power
concepts.description: 400-hectare fortress, ~70 meter pyramid; 100+ female skulls beneath East Gate; violence evidence; shaman-king cosmology; ritual consolidation hypothesis; comparative human sacrifice (Egypt, Mesopotamia, China, Mesoamerica)

concepts.title: Longshan Oracle Bones Divination
concepts.description: Systematization of shamanic knowledge; inscribe question → apply heat → read cracks; standardized protocol; elite family control; Shang continuation with recording of outcomes; transition from charismatic to procedural authority

title: Neolithic and Prehistoric Violence (Stone Age Herbalist — Demon Bats & Cocaine Mummies Vol. III, 2026)

concepts.title: Prehistoric Fortifications Eurasia LBK Amnya
concepts.description: LBK farmers (~5,500-4,500 BCE) and Amnya foragers (~6,500-4,500 BCE) both built fortifications; massacre sites (Talheim, Schöneck, Asparn, Halberstadt, Herxheim); ~10% of LBK skeletons show traumatic injuries; 8.2kya climate event driving territoriality; warfare correlates with settlement density, not subsistence type

concepts.title: Vittrup Man Mesolithic Slavery and Sacrifice
concepts.description: 1915 bog body discovery; 2024 genetic/isotopic analysis; maritime diet (seal/cod/whale) birth to age 13, then inland farmer diet; eight blunt-force skull blows (wooden club); ritual execution/sacrifice evidence; evidence of prehistoric slavery

concepts.title: Ritual Violence and State Formation
concepts.description: Human sacrifice at city founding, fortress construction, elite burial, military victory, political succession; absolute power demonstration; cosmological legitimation; captives, enemies, slaves as victims; transition to bureaucratic execution

title: Demographic and Population Studies (Stone Age Herbalist — Demon Bats & Cocaine Mummies Vol. III, 2026)

concepts.title: Demographic Collapse Parsi
concepts.description: Population decline 100,000 (1950s) → 69,601 (2001) → ~60,000 (2011); 1-2% annual decline; fertility 0.8 vs. replacement 2.1; median age 42-43; extinction timeline 80-120 years; fertility collapse from education, economic opportunity, delayed marriage, voluntary childlessness; out-marriage 40-50% in diaspora; religious endogamy and no-conversion restrictions; aging population and generational collapse; diclofenac/vulture extinction disrupting sky burial; religious inflexibility

concepts.title: Tasmania Aboriginal Dietary Collapse
concepts.description: 43,000-10,000 years ago megafauna present; 10,000-3,500 years ago transition period; 3,500 years ago to contact restricted diet; ocean-going technology abandonment; climate change 3,500 years ago; dietary narrowing and technological loss; isolation hypothesis

concepts.title: Iceland Decodegen Genetics Patenting
concepts.description: 1,000-year population isolation; 1998 health sector license granting access to genetic samples, genealogical data, medical records; consent and re-identification controversies; 2003 reforms (opt-out, profit-sharing); discovery outcomes; genetic patriotism framing; comparison to genetic colonialism

title: Maritime Power and Trade Networks (Stone Age Herbalist — Demon Bats & Cocaine Mummies Vol. III, 2026)

concepts.title: Thalassocracy and Maritime Power
concepts.description: Maritime dominance as political power; prerequisites (ship design, navigation, infrastructure, naval capability); tributary networks and monopoly pricing; spice trade example; exploration and first-mover advantage

concepts.title: Tuunbaq Mythology Predator Symbol
concepts.description: Predatory entity in Inuit cosmology; Arctic predators (polar bears, orca whales, walrus); composite threat encoding; predator as cosmic principle; humans as prey in Arctic environment; ritual protocols for hunting and avoiding predators; mythology as psychological framework

title: Spanish Conquest of Mexico (Townsend / *The Fifth Sun*, 2003)

concepts.title: Conquest as Negotiated Process
concepts.description: Conquest was contingent on specific indigenous choices (Marina's translation, Moctezuma's negotiation, Tlaxcaltec alliance) not inevitable Spanish military superiority; Spanish military advantage was real but made decisive only through indigenous political divisions and pragmatic choices; Spanish success required indigenous labor, intermediaries, and continued functioning of indigenous institutions

concepts.title: Marina as Indispensable Agent of Conquest
concepts.description: Enslaved indigenous woman whose translation made Spanish conquest possible; translation was essential cognitive labor constructing meaning across incommensurable worldviews; systematic erasure from conquest narratives reveals how translator labor is made invisible and uncompensated; Marina's invisibility serves ideological function maintaining Spanish narrative authority

concepts.title: Smallpox as Force Multiplier Not Cause
concepts.description: Epidemic disease cascading through multiple systems simultaneously (succession crises, labor disruption, burial obligations, decision-making paralysis); smallpox enabled conquest but did not determine it; simultaneous disruption at social, political, and economic levels created crisis management failure

concepts.title: Moctezuma's Pragmatism Under Radical Uncertainty
concepts.description: Pragmatic negotiating strategy in response to unprecedented threat, incomplete intelligence, institutional constraints; negotiation was rational choice minimizing catastrophic risk while preserving institutional structures; choice enabled Spanish to exploit political divisions and achieve conquest

concepts.title: Xiuhpohualli: 52-Year Indigenous Calendar as Political Framework
concepts.description: Cyclical time structure framing conquest as disruption within ongoing cycle rather than apocalypse; indigenous use of calendar as resistance to Spanish linear chronology; temporal autonomy as political assertion

concepts.title: Altepetl: Indigenous City-States as Political Units
concepts.description: Altepetl as persistent administrative units surviving conquest through Spanish pragmatic preservation; Spanish preserved structures because they were functionally efficient; indigenous institutions became foundation of colonial administration

concepts.title: Calpolli: Decentralized Administration Through Kinship Structure
concepts.description: Kinship-based labor organization as remarkably efficient administrative technology; Spanish preserved calpolli because colonial system depended on it; indigenous people maintained structures partly for community preservation, partly enabling Spanish extraction through their own institutions

concepts.title: Tlatoani: Paramount Chief as Negotiator Under Constraint
concepts.description: Tlatoani preserved elite identity by negotiating position within Spanish colonial hierarchy; pragmatic choice for status preservation despite loss of sovereignty; elite adaptation demonstrates how institutional roles survive political disruption

concepts.title: Flower Wars (Xochiyaoyotl): Ritualized Conflict as Sophisticated Statecraft
concepts.description: Ritualized military practice appearing as weakness in Spanish frame but wisdom in indigenous frame; Spanish refusal to follow protocols prevented indigenous ability to communicate military capacity through established channels; system collapsed not from indigenous weakness but from Spanish protocol refusal

concepts.title: Chimalpahin's Annals: Indigenous Historiography as Archive and Assertion
concepts.description: Indigenous historian using documentation as resistance strategy; preserving indigenous perspective, genealogy, and knowledge against Spanish colonization threat; archive as political act

title: Spanish Conquest and Colonial Analysis (Townsend / *The Fifth Sun*, 2023)

concepts.title: Translator Invisibility: The Hidden Scaffolding of Historic Change
concepts.description: translation labor foundational yet structurally invisible; Marina's case as paradigm: absolutely essential and systematically erased; invisibility serves ideological function for colonial narratives of autonomous European agency; behavioral economics of invisible cognitive labor

concepts.title: Agency vs. Structure: How Individual Choices Navigate Institutional Inevitability
concepts.description: agency and structure not opposites; structure sets possible options, agency selects which to pursue; conquest contingent on specific indigenous choices (Marina's translation, Moctezuma's negotiation, Tlaxcaltec alliance) within structural constraints (Spanish military advantage)

concepts.title: Pragmatism vs. Ideology: How Practical Choices Get Reinterpreted as Inevitable Beliefs
concepts.description: pragmatic choices systematically reinterpreted as ideological stances by outside observers with narrative authority; Moctezuma's negotiation reframed from rational-response-to-constraint to fatalistic-surrender; interpretation transforms contingent outcomes into inevitable consequences of indigenous beliefs

concepts.title: Archive as Resistance: Documentation as Political Act Under Colonialism
concepts.description: archive not neutral preservation but political choice about whose perspective matters; Chimalpahin's Nahuatl annals as resistance — indigenous documentation in form Spanish crown recognized while preserving indigenous meaning structures; archive as assertion of persistence and legitimacy

concepts.title: Agricultural Development Timing: How Material Preconditions Enable or Constrain Political Possibility
concepts.description: sophisticated agricultural development (chinampas, tribute networks) created material foundation enabling Mexica civilization AND vulnerability Spanish exploited; path dependence in agricultural systems constrained strategic options under Spanish invasion; sophistication as simultaneous advantage and liability

title: Farmer-Forager Contact (Stone Age Herbalist / *Demon Bats & Cocaine Mummies Vol. III*, 2023)

concepts.title: Farmer-Forager Contact Dynamics: From Narrative of Progress to Ecology of Choice
concepts.description: three divergent outcomes from farmer-forager contact (Corded Ware Baltic: de-neolithisation; Drakensberg: hybridity; Mediterranean: coexistence); outcomes depend on ecology and cultural choice, not agricultural superiority as universal principle; agricultural progress not inevitable

concepts.title: Western Xia False Retreat & Learning from Opponent Errors
concepts.description: Khan's adaptive learning from tactical defeat; false retreat as strategic mechanism; meritocracy enabling innovation

concepts.title: Horse-Milking Logistics & Supply Chain
concepts.description: Elimination of supply trains through horse-milking; systematic optimization of speed and sustainability; organizational discipline enabling logistics innovation

concepts.title: Khwarezm Invasion: Merchant Murder & Accidental Escalation
concepts.description: Merchant embassy murder triggering total war; escalation spiral and reputational necessity; paranoia creating inevitable response

concepts.title: Disease, Death Tolls & Inflated Narratives
concepts.description: Plague and disease vs. direct military killing; exaggerated death toll narratives; propaganda function of destruction reputation

title: Core Strategic Concepts

concepts.title: Dual Strategic Modes
concepts.description: Ability to shift from cavalry-charge pursuit to siege-position strategy based on problem type identification; flexibility as strategic advantage; lost after Hyphasis when paranoia locks Alexander into control-seeking only

concepts.title: Core Problem Identification
concepts.description: Alexander's method: identify the structural weakness that makes the system collapse; attack that point regardless of apparent strength or defensive fortifications; works perfectly until problem misidentification occurs

concepts.title: Cutting vs. Untying: When to Destroy the Puzzle
concepts.description: Distinction between destruction (faster, sacrifices elegance, solves immediate problem) and solution (maintains system, preserves relationships); clarity lies in knowing which solves the actual problem not merely the puzzle; Gordian Knot as canonical example

concepts.title: Morale as Strategic Weapon
concepts.description: Battlefield force multiplier with material effects; army collapse without physical defeat when morale center breaks; Alexander's method: target the psychological command center (the king) to cascade morale collapse across entire force; visible command presence determines group cohesion

concepts.title: Bucephalus and Observational Problem-Solving
concepts.description: Pattern recognition and reframing as problem-solving method; see the real constraint beneath the apparent constraint; observation goes deeper than obvious problem; applies across Bucephalus (shadow fear), Gordian Knot (functional goal), and Issus (Darius vulnerability)

concepts.title: Aristotle vs. Leonidas
concepts.description: Education as personality foundation; Leonidas teaches will-imposition and physical mastery; Aristotle teaches observation and logical clarity; merger creates distinctive cognitive style; tension between the two tutors becomes visible at Hyphasis

concepts.title: Granicus and Parmenion's Caution
concepts.description: Early pattern of Alexander overriding careful strategy with aggressive clarity; direct assault instead of flanking; Alexander's approach succeeds, establishing authority over senior general; success creates overconfidence about risk-taking across domains

title: The Trajectory of Achievement and Collapse

concepts.title: Achievement Vanity and Corrosion
concepts.description: Pursuing objectives for personal magnitude rather than strategic necessity; corrosive to decision-making when cost grows; Gedrosian Desert crossing kills 25,000+ soldiers to validate Alexander's exceptionality; vanity as psychological engine of destructive choices

concepts.title: Siwa Oracle and Orchestrated Validation
concepts.description: Need for external validation of exceptionality; orchestrated validation cannot satisfy because source of doubt isn't removed—it's reinforced; moment validation becomes obvious as performance, it collapses as validation; existential trap when identity requires external confirmation

concepts.title: Gaugamela and the Morale-First Refusal
concepts.description: Alexander refuses tactical advantage (defensive position) for morale certainty (direct charge at Darius); overrides Parmenion's caution; accepts high casualties for psychological victory; demonstrates clarity about what matters (morale collapse) vs. apparent obstacles (numerical disadvantage)

concepts.title: Peace Offer Refusal and Total Victory
concepts.description: Repeated refusal of negotiated peace; commitment to absolute conquest over pragmatic victory; refusal of all compromise establishes different authority structure; total victory creates no space for shared power; creates paranoia when cultural resistance emerges (cannot negotiate with culture the way you can with kings)

concepts.title: Hyphasis Collective Refusal
concepts.description: Will-imposition hits structural limit when collective withdraws consent; cannot force 40,000 soldiers without destroying the army; collective will proven stronger than individual will when leverage exists; trauma response when identity built on will-imposition meets immovable object

concepts.title: The India Refusal at Hyphasis
concepts.description: Army's structural refusal to continue into India; not psychological weakness but physical/logistical boundary; distinction between overcoming psychological fear and overcoming structural exhaustion; identity-level trauma when will-imposition encounters irreversible limit

title: Paranoia Escalation and Control-Seeking

concepts.title: Sequential Paranoia
concepts.description: Emotional dyscontrol (Cleitus murder) → instrumental elimination (Philotas execution) → enforcement (Callisthenes killing) → systematic control; paranoia as trauma response to will-imposition hitting structural limit; moment killing truth-tellers becomes necessary, paranoia trap closes irreversibly

concepts.title: Philotas Trial and Calculated Paranoia
concepts.description: Paranoia organized into strategy; suspicion deployed as justification for eliminating rivals; use of trial process to legitimize execution; distinction between emotional paranoia (genuine fear) and instrumental paranoia (using suspicion as tool); escalation dynamic where paranoia performed becomes paranoia genuine

concepts.title: Mass Marriages as Failed Cultural Mandate
concepts.description: Institutional attempt to create cultural unity through ordered marriage; marriages happen but resistance persists; culture cannot be mandated through decree when lived identity resists fusion; institutional action produces institutional results, not cultural transformation

concepts.title: Proskynesis as Failed Cultural Bridge
concepts.description: Ritual prostration as attempted synthesis of Greek and Persian legitimacy; fails because both sides recognize it as performance, not genuine fusion; ritual cannot carry political weight when consciousness of performance is present; meaning cannot be mandated

concepts.title: Institutional Continuity vs. Cultural Fusion
concepts.description: Institutions (bureaucracies, tax systems) survive change of rulers; cultures (lived meaning, identity) cannot be unified by decree; paradox: institutional success masking cultural failure; Babylon works, Persepolis burning fails, later mandates impossible

title: Models of Conquest: Extraction, Preservation, and Destruction

concepts.title: Babylon Institutional Model
concepts.description: Non-intervention as strategy: inherit existing machinery, change only who's at top, let system continue functioning; form-dependent institutions work under new authority without restructuring; Alexander's institutional best; contrasts with later confusion about culture

concepts.title: Susa Treasury and Extractive Sustainability
concepts.description: Capture resources (treasury) while maintaining system that generates them (institutions); intermediate between pure extraction and pure preservation; more effective than extraction alone; shift from resource-extraction (Susa) to control-seeking (paranoia) marks structural change

concepts.title: Persepolis Burned
concepts.description: Destruction of dynastic symbols (coronation sites, sacred spaces); dynasty cannot be inherited but institutions can; clarity decision to end era; contrasts with later confusion about cultural fusion; strategic clarity about what must be destroyed vs. preserved

title: The Founder-Consolidator Problem

concepts.title: "To the Strongest" and Succession Failure
concepts.description: Founder dies without designating successor; ambiguous statement invites civil war; absence of institutional succession mechanism ensures collapse when personality gone; Diadochi Wars fragment empire; founder-problem structural to empire-building—founding requires will-imposition, consolidation requires shared power

title: Consolidation Timeline & Comparative Patterns (5 pages)

concepts.title: Consolidation Timeline (1872-1901)
concepts.description: Five phases over 29 years showing specific decisions at critical moments. Key transition points: 1872 entry, 1872 Frick partnership, 1893 panic deployment, 1895 emergence, 1901 Morgan sale. Demonstrates sequencing was critical; delay would have produced different outcomes.

concepts.title: Gilded Age Consolidation Pattern
concepts.description: Same five-step pattern (fragmentation → foundation → crisis → integration → dominance) repeated across steel, oil, railroads, banking. Demonstrates consolidation is structural, not genius-dependent. Enabling conditions: capital availability, crisis frequency, low antitrust enforcement, scale advantages. Implication: consolidation predictable when conditions align.

concepts.title: Industrial Consolidation as Selection
concepts.description: Consolidation driven by structural selection environment. Selection pressure: cost structure (primary), capital strength (secondary), managerial excellence (tertiary). Example: steel 1870-1900, dozens fragmented → 1893 crisis selects for fitness → Carnegie emerges dominant.

concepts.title: Civil War as Capital Redistribution
concepts.description: Civil War created capital accumulation conditions enabling Carnegie's 1860s wealth. Military demand surge + post-war reconstruction + cheap labor. War-era capital enabled 1872 entry into steel, compressing passive income timeline from ~1880s to 1872.

concepts.title: Recession Consolidation
concepts.description: Recessions function as selection filters: Collapse (weak fail), Survive Weakened (moderate shrink), Consolidate & Expand (strong with capital reserves win). 1873 and 1893 panics: Carnegie with capital reserves survived and consolidated; competitors without reserves collapsed or sold.

title: Thucydides Trap Recognition (1 page)

concepts.title: Thucydides Trap Recognition
concepts.description: Dominant power recognizes rising power and accommodates via exit/cooperation. Carnegie recognized Morgan's threat (1899-1900), exited at premium ($480M) rather than fighting U.S. Steel. Strategic accommodation preserved capital for philanthropy and reputation. Fighting the trap leads to worse outcomes.

title: Core History Pages (Biographical & Strategic Foundation)

concepts.title: Hamilcar Barca: State-Builder and Bloodline Transfer
concepts.description: State-builder mentality; Spanish pivot strategy; oath-binding transmission across generations; psychological lock transmitted to 9-year-old Hannibal

concepts.title: Hannibal: The Oath-Bound Strategist
concepts.description: Oath binds individual to irrational goal (destroy Rome); brilliant strategist constrained by irrationality of objective; tension between rational military genius and irrational civilizational commitment

concepts.title: Rome's Post-Cannae Resilience: Irrationality as Strength
concepts.description: Rome refuses negotiation after Cannae; commits to indefinite war; willingness to ruin allies and conscript slaves to maintain war capacity; irrationality as strategic advantage

concepts.title: Scipio Africanus: The Student Who Defeated the Master
concepts.description: Learned Hannibal's principles; applied Rome's attrition strategy instead of competing at tempo; asymmetric response to tempo strategy; system-level advantage over individual genius

title: Major Engagements (Battles & Tactical Moments)

concepts.title: Trebia River: First Blood
concepts.description: Opening engagement establishing Hannibal's principles in practice; intelligence dominance, positioning, tempo convergence; Rome loses 20,000; pattern established

concepts.title: Lake Trasimene: Ambush Masterclass
concepts.description: Deception at scale; fake retreat triggers Rome's expected pursuit; Rome walks into valley surrounded by hills; 15,000 Roman casualties; ambush architecture weaponization

concepts.title: The Arno Marshes: Survival Through Maneuver and Shared Sacrifice
concepts.description: Rome attempts to trap Hannibal in marshes; Hannibal crosses impossible terrain; shared hardship at breaking point; survival itself becomes victory when alternative is annihilation

concepts.title: The Cannae Inversion: Opponent's Strength Becomes Vulnerability
concepts.description: Rome's numerical superiority becomes vulnerability when positioned in Hannibal's crescent; envelopment; 50,000 Roman casualties; overwhelming victory triggers Rome's irrationality

concepts.title: Cannae: The Perfect Envelopment
concepts.description: Double envelopment as textbook example; crescent formation; the snap when Rome realizes it's enclosed; paradox of tactical perfection becoming strategic disaster

concepts.title: The Alps Crossing: Impossibility as Weapon
concepts.description: Audacious crossing of route Rome assumes impassable; 20,000 casualties but army intact; psychological dominance through demonstration that Rome's assumptions are wrong

concepts.title: Zama: When Speed Meets Time
concepts.description: Hannibal forced to return to Africa; Scipio does not match Hannibal's tactical brilliance; Rome's attrition strategy has succeeded; Hannibal's speed advantage has degraded to irrelevance

title: Consolidation & Stalemate Phase

concepts.title: Hannibal in Italy: Consolidation and Stalemate
concepts.description: Post-Cannae pivot from winning battles to controlling territory; different principles required for consolidation; Rome's institutional authority better suited to territory control; Hannibal's speed principles less effective

concepts.title: Numidian Cavalry: Force Advantage and Defection
concepts.description: Most effective cavalry force Rome has encountered; tactical dominance; strategic vulnerability when victories stop coming; Massinissa's defection demonstrates heterogeneous force fragility

title: Hannibal's Operating Principles (Behavioral-Mechanics in History Context)

concepts.title: Intelligence Dominance as Force Multiplier
concepts.description: Systematic information asymmetry; scout networks; enables accurate positioning

concepts.title: Tempo Control: Speed as Strategic Weapon
concepts.description: Speed of movement, decision-making, adaptation; perpetual advantage until opponent learns to predict

concepts.title: Surprise as Demoralization: Psychological Weapon Disguised as Tactic
concepts.description: Expectation violation; expectation shattered at breaking point

concepts.title: Audacious Goals and High-Risk Strategy
concepts.description: Attempting impossible; forcing opponent outside prepared frameworks

concepts.title: Shared Hardship as Loyalty Mechanism
concepts.description: Leader's body as organizational asset; shared suffering transcends rational cost-benefit

concepts.title: Deception and Misdirection Tactics
concepts.description: Controlled misbelief; observable facts interpreted falsely

concepts.title: Ambush Architecture: Terrain as Weaponization
concepts.description: Geography as constraint; terrain as weapon

concepts.title: Decision Velocity: How Command Structure Determines Tactical Response Speed
concepts.description: Centralized vs. distributed command; Hannibal's fast, Rome's slow

concepts.title: Army Composition: Heterogeneous Force as Adaptive Weapon
concepts.description: Diversity enables rapid adaptation; creates fragility when motivation erodes

concepts.title: Logistics and Extended Campaign: Sustaining Indefinite War
concepts.description: Predatory economics as logistics doctrine; fragile when victories stop

concepts.title: Encirclement Tactics: Psychological Warfare
concepts.description: Agency loss as weapon; surrounding force; psychological effect disproportionate to military effect

concepts.title: The Incomplete Victory Problem: Winning Battles, Losing Wars
concepts.description: Oversuccess triggers opponent irrationality; optimal victory magnitude exists below which opponent remains rational

title: Cross-Domain Collisions (Strategic & Philosophical Level)

concepts.title: Tempo vs. Attrition: Speed Against Infinite Time
concepts.description: Core strategic collision; three phases (Tempo Dominance, Attrition Accumulation, Attrition Victory); incompatible optimization strategies on different time horizons

concepts.title: Genius vs. System: Why Individual Brilliance Loses to Institutional Resilience
concepts.description: Hannibal's individual genius unable to overcome Rome's institutional system; system wins when timeline extends beyond individual capacity

concepts.title: Rationality vs. Sacred Commitment: When Irrationality Wins
concepts.description: Rome's civilizational commitment to survival beats Hannibal's rational military strategy; framework shift from cost-benefit to identity commitment

concepts.title: Intelligence and Prediction of Irrational Behavior
concepts.description: Intelligence dominance fails when opponent abandons rationality; information advantage framework-dependent

concepts.title: Victory Magnitude and Opponent Irrationality Threshold
concepts.description: Optimal victory magnitude exists; too complete victory triggers opponent irrationality; oversuccess becomes strategic liability

title: Warfare Evolution & Historical Patterns

concepts.title: Evolution of Warfare: From Tribal Skirmish to State Apparatus
concepts.description: Transformation from personal, visible tribal raiding (bounded scale, intimate violence) to state-organized warfare (diffused responsibility, industrial atrocity). Institutional machinery enables violence at unprecedented scale while diffusing moral responsibility. State scaling of tribal behavioral systems.

concepts.title: War and the State of Nature: Hobbes, Pinker, and Human Violence
concepts.description: Critique of "savage state of nature" narrative as post-hoc justification for state power rather than description of human nature. Anthropological evidence of diverse violence rates across societies reveals violence as conditional on social organization, not fixed nature. Narrative shapes reality through self-fulfilling prophecy.

title: Genocide & Atrocity Mechanisms

concepts.title: Rwanda Genocide: How Arbitrary Categories Become Killing Boundaries (EXPANDED)
concepts.description: Case study integrating minimal-group-paradigm boundary activation, dehumanization metaphor propaganda, embodied cognition through media saturation, threat activation, organizational machinery, and diffusion of responsibility into single coherent genocide mechanism. Demonstrates how multiple behavioral systems align to enable atrocity. Temporal ordering: propaganda → militia mobilization → genocide.

concepts.title: The Historical Pattern: Why Dehumanization Precedes Atrocity
concepts.description: Documenting temporal ordering across multiple atrocity cases (Rwanda, Nazi Germany, slavery, Native American genocide): dehumanization propaganda comes first, systematic killing follows. Dehumanization is neurobiological prerequisite, not post-hoc justification. Prevention strategy: interrupt dehumanization before it becomes ubiquitous.

title: Peace-Making & Reconciliation

concepts.title: Peace-Making as Symbolic Recognition: Structural Conditions for Genuine Coexistence
concepts.description: Material peace agreements fail to heal without symbolic recognition of other group's humanity. Symbolic concessions (King Hussein's "brother" at Rabin's funeral, McGuinness' handshake at Robinson's vulnerability, Mandela's Afrikaans-speaking diplomacy) reverse dehumanization through same neurobiological channels by which dehumanization was created. Requires authentic recognition, moment of vulnerability or transition, understanding of sacred values.

title: Soviet and Korean War Coercion

concepts.title: Pavlov, Show Trials, and Soviet Interrogation Doctrine
concepts.description: Transmarginal collapse as Lenin's applied political technology; Moscow show trials as demonstration; Bukharin's philosophically sophisticated confession; doctrine transfer east and west; Pavlov's own opposition

concepts.title: Korean War Brainwashing Panic
concepts.description: What actually happened: standard DDD coercive conditions; panic was misinterpretation (7,000 captured; 21 remained; 15-30% collaboration); 3 institutional uses of brainwashing narrative (exculpatory/threat-amplifying/self-protective); SERE as ordinary-person-thesis operationalized as training
